calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Nov-19-2014 Vacated

Notice Of Motion To Disqualify Pltf'S Counsel
Off Calendar Oct-02-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. MATTHEW K. YAN OF THE MOSCONE EMBLIDGE SATER & OTIS, TEL. NO. 362-3599 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. BABAK YOUSEFZADEH OF SHEPPARD MULLIN LLP, TEL. NO. 434-9100 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS BENTLY HOLDINGS AND CALIFORNIA LIMITED PARTNERSHIP AND CHRISTOPHER BENTLY'S Motion To Disqualify Plaintiff's Counsel is denied. Defendants' delay in bringing this Motion was unreasonable and the ensuing prejudice to Plaintiff if this Motion was granted is extreme. While Defendants may have warned Plaintiff's counsel that its actions were grounds for disqualification one day after the Complaint was filed, the Court was not notified that Defendants believed the actions required disqualification until over a year later. Furthermore, if Defendants believed that Plaintiff's counsel was so tainted by its possession of privileged material, it is inconceivable that Defendants would have chosen to engage in settlement discussions while Plaintiff was represented by the same counsel. Finally, Plaintiff has shown that he would be severely prejudiced if Defendants' Motion was granted due to additional delay in the litigation of his claim on its merits. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/eg

Defendants Bently Holdings California Limited Partnership And Christopher Bently'S Motion For Protective Order Re Privileged Communications
Off Calendar Mar-07-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES: MATTHEW K. YAN, ESQ., 415.362.3599, FOR PLAINTIFF ERIK PARKER.
BABEK YOUSEFZADEH, ESQ., 415.434.9100, FOR DEFENDANTS BENTLY HOLDINGS CALIFORNIA LIMITED PARTNERSHIP AND CHRISTOPHER BENTLY.

RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. Defendants Bently Holdings California Limited Partnership And Christopher Bently's Motion For Protective Order Re Privileged Communications is granted. The Court finds that communications (principally emails) between plaintiff Erik Parker and Bently Holdings California Limited Partnership (BHCLP) attorney Michael Donner in connection with the Papaliolios matter fall under the attorney-client privilege. These communications include the various versions of the Erik Parker declaration prepared by counsel, unless they have been made public. Erik Parker had the title Security Manager, and supervised BHCLP security personnel. When attorney Donner communicated with Erik Parker seeking his input for his declaration in the Papaliolios litigation, it was expected in plaintiff Parker's capacity as Security Manager that he was required to make statements on behalf of BHCLP. See D.I. Chadbourne, Inc. v. Sup.Ct. (1964) 60 Cal.2d 723 at 736-739. The same rationale applies to Samuel Calpo, who, although not a manager, was allegedly working security at the event underlying the Papaliolios litigation, and would be required to provide statements to BHCLP as a member of the security staff.
Communications (involving the Papaliolios matter) between attorney Donner and Brady Frey of BHCLP, and between attorney Donner and any other employees of BHCLP (such as Anne Hill, Samuel Calpo, and "Vivian") are also protected from discovery under the attorney-client privilege. The Court finds that the crime-fraud exception to the attorney-client privilege does not apply. There is insufficient evidence proffered that BHCLP sought or obtained legal assistance "to enable or aid" it to commit a crime or fraud. Evidence Code sec. 956; Geilim v. Superior Court (1991) 234 Cal.App.3d 166, 174.
The Court orders plaintiff and his counsel to return to defense counsel within 10 days of the entry of this Order all copies, including electronic versions, of the above-referenced attorney-client privileged material which plaintiff and/or counsel has in his/her possession, custody or control. The Court also issues a Protective Order precluding plaintiff from seeking in discovery the production or content of attorney-client privileged communications made in the Papaliolios matter. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE PRO TEM CHARLES GEERHART, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, NOT REPORTED.
